page: Google Cloud Platform z3-highmem-22-standardlssd is a memory-optimized server instance featuring 22 vCPUs and 176 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor operating at 2.7 GHz, this instance provides 11 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The hardware configuration includes 6442 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, offering high-capacity, low-latency local storage without any bundled GPUs. The CPU architecture is x86_64, featuring advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix operations.

In benchmark evaluations, the z3-highmem-22-standardlssd demonstrates solid performance across memory and compute workloads. It achieves a Geekbench multi-core score of 11688.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 27968.71. Memory performance is highlighted by a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 1495.22 GB/sec and a large block uncached write bandwidth of 42.11 GB/sec. For specialized workloads, the instance achieves 23.89 million operations per second in Redis SET operations and a top-tier text generation speed of 321.65 tokens per second using a small 135M LLM model, leveraging its AMX instruction set.

This instance offers a balanced qualitative cost-efficiency profile for memory-intensive and storage-heavy workloads by bundling substantial NVMe SSD capacity directly with a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The absence of a GPU keeps compute costs focused on CPU and memory resources. It is highly suitable for large-scale database deployments, in-memory caching systems like Redis, data processing pipelines, and localized machine learning inference tasks that benefit from Intel AMX instructions.
description: Google Cloud Platform z3-highmem-22-standardlssd is a memory-optimized server configured with 22 vCPUs and 176 GB of RAM, yielding an 8 GB per vCPU ratio. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C x86_64 processor running at 2.7 GHz across 11 physical cores. The instance features 6442 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, providing high-speed local data access, though it lacks dedicated GPU hardware. Performance benchmarks show a Passmark CPU score of 27968.71, a Geekbench multi-core score of 11688.0, and a Redis SET throughput of 23.89 million operations per second. It also exhibits strong text generation capabilities, reaching 321.65 tokens per second on a small LLM model. This server is highly efficient for memory-intensive databases, caching layers, and data processing workloads requiring large local storage.

tagline: A memory-optimized server featuring high-capacity local NVMe storage and advanced Intel Xeon processing for demanding database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 22 vCPUs and 176 GB of RAM providing an 8 GB per vCPU memory-optimized ratio.
6442 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage for low-latency data access.
Intel Xeon 8481C processor with AVX-512 and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX).
Delivers 23.89 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmark testing.
Best suited for in-memory caching, large databases, and data processing.
